claim: Cost effectiveness evaluations based on cRCT data lack external validity.
evidence_sentences: Only a minority of patients used the drugs long-term and daily (34.5% of conventional NSAIDs and 44.2% of coxibs), whereas coxib RCTs required daily use for at least 6-9 months.
The mean costs (for GPRD events) over calendar time were US$58k during 1990-1993 and US$174k during 2002-2005.
Using RCT data rather than GPRD data for event probabilities, the mean cost was US$16k with the VIGOR RCT and US$20k with the CLASS RCT.

CONCLUSIONS The published cost-effectiveness analyses of coxibs lacked external validity, did not represent patients in actual clinical practice, and should not have been used to inform prescribing policies.
